title: Preamble
lead: ==‘Identity Card’ (N. Palkhivala)==, ==‘Political Horoscope’ (K. M. Munshi)==. First to be moved in the Constituent Assembly as the Objectives Resolution, last to be added to the final draft — the Preamble witnessed evolution even before it was born.
intro: Though it has no independent legal status of its own, the Preamble has been used to derive constitutional intent in seminal cases — Property Owners, Electoral Bonds, EWS Reservation, Sabarimala, Privacy.

items.title: Sovereign
bullets: ==Op. Sindoor==, surgical strikes, Galwan Valley, development of nuclear power, Rohingya resettlement
==Doctrine of Parens Patriae== — State as parent

items.title: Socialist
bullets: FDI, PLI, MGNREGA, Lakhpati Didi, food security, housing, RTE, PM-JAY, ONDC

items.title: Secular
bullets: Triple talaq, Waqf, ==Sabarimala==, conversions, management of temples/shrines
Government funding for major religious events

items.title: Justice
bullets: ==Social== — affirmative action, reservation in education & employment, SC/ST atrocities
==Political== — legislative reservation for women, SC, ST

items.title: Equality
bullets: ==EBL + EPL== (equality before law + equal protection of laws)

items.title: Individual Dignity
bullets: Strict ==anti-lynching provisions== in the new BNS

blocks.text: The Basic Structure case ==sourced most ingredients of the doctrine from the Preamble's objectives== — the Preamble can be amended, but not in a manner that violates those objectives (basic structure).

sections.title: Utility — the ‘Key to the Makers' Minds’

blocks.text: The Preamble is the key to open the mind of the makers of this beautiful Constitution.

items.title: Constitutional Interpretation
bullets: [[Property Owners Assn v. State of MH (2024)]] — not all privately owned property is a ==‘material resource of the community’==.

items.title: Statutory Interpretation
bullets: [[ADR v. UoI (2024)]] — ==Electoral Bonds== struck down; democracy as the touchstone.

items.title: International Ratification
bullets: [[Vishaka v. State of RJ (1997)]] — ==CEDAW== used to frame workplace harassment guidelines.
